title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in a viral video by Watertown's Brian Daly, known for judging bars by their Bob Seger playlist.
Healey joined Daly at Beacon Hill's 21st Amendment bar, requesting Seger's "Still the Same" and awarding a 'certified Seger bar' sticker.
Daly first met Healey at a union anniversary event and later encountered her by chance while filming, leading to her cameo.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings to open its first Massachusetts location in Framingham at 84 Worcester Rd.
The Framingham site will replace the former Fresh Market, adding to Nan Xiang's 10 locations and nine planned openings.
Nan Xiang is known for handmade soup dumplings, with menu offerings including traditional dim sum and noodles.

title: ‘The Drama’ review: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson’s darkly funny Boston movie is designed to discomfort
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Drama', starring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, is a Boston-based dark comedy exploring societal taboos and relationships.
Directed by Kristoffer Borgli, the film critiques social norms with a storyline involving personal confessions before a wedding.
The film premieres in theaters on April 3, showcasing performances by Zendaya, Pattinson, Mamoudou Athie, and Alana Haim.

title: National UMass survey has bad news for Trump
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: University of Massachusetts Amherst poll shows Trump's approval rating at 33%, the lowest of his second term.
The poll highlights declining support among key groups, with a 20-point drop among men, working-class, and Black voters.
Economic management criticisms, disapproval of foreign policy, and concerns about immigration impact Trump's declining approval.

title: Globe Magazine names New England’s 50 fastest-growing companies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: The Boston Globe and Statista named New England's 50 fastest-growing companies in their yearly collaboration.
Businesses spanned sectors like medical equipment, fintech, hospitality, and restaurants; requirements included $1.5 million revenue by 2024.
Despite economic challenges, these companies achieved significant growth, highlighting resilience in a troubled economic climate.

title: Judge denies Lindsay Clancy’s request for two-part murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge William Sullivan denied Lindsay Clancy's request for a two-phase murder trial in Plymouth Superior Court.
Lindsay Clancy faces trial in July for allegedly killing her three children in Duxbury in January 2023.
Clancy, accused of murder, claims postpartum mental illness and auditory hallucinations; a single trial deemed more efficient.

title: What we know about the armed robbery of a massive Beverly mansion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A masked intruder assaulted a caretaker and stole valuables from the 'Rock Edge' mansion in Beverly on March 28.
The suspect, who is still at large, fled in a stolen Porsche later recovered by police, according to Chief John LeLacheur.
The historic Beverly mansion is known for its notable history and was purchased in 2023 for $18,275,000.

title: Walmart closes Worcester fulfillment center, laying off 90 employees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Walmart closed its Sam’s Club Fulfillment Center in Worcester on February 25, laying off 90 employees.
Laid-off employees have a 90-day paid transition period to find new positions within Walmart or Sam's Club.
Affected employees can receive a $7,500 transfer incentive and relocation benefits for eligible positions over 50 miles away.

title: Long-planned Nubian Square jazz club finally secures funding
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Jazz Urbane Cafe secures $2.5 million to open in Roxbury's Nubian Square.
Funding includes $380,000 equipment loan from MassDevelopment and $1.63 million loan from M&T Bank.
The venue will be on the first floor of Bruce C. Bolling Municipal Building, seating 200 guests.

title: JetBlue has raised checked bag fees. See how much you’ll pay.
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: JetBlue increased checked bag fees by $4 during off-peak and $9 during peak travel periods.
For U.S., Latin America, Caribbean, and Canada flights, checked bag fees now start at $39 for off-peak and $49 for peak.
JetBlue Plus and TrueBlue Mosaic members still receive free checked bags under current benefits policies.
